_comment: Kibana alerting rules for FTGO log-based error rate monitoring

alerts.name: ftgo-high-error-rate
alerts.description: Triggers when the error rate across any FTGO service exceeds 5% of total log volume in a 5-minute window

notify.message: High error rate detected in service {{context.service}}: {{context.value}} errors in the last 5 minutes

alerts.name: ftgo-critical-error-spike
alerts.description: Triggers when a single service produces more than 200 ERROR logs in a 5-minute window, indicating a critical failure

notify.message: CRITICAL: Error spike in service {{context.service}}: {{context.value}} errors in the last 5 minutes. Immediate investigation required.

alerts.name: ftgo-service-log-silence
alerts.description: Triggers when a service stops producing logs for more than 10 minutes, which may indicate the service is down

notify.message: Service {{context.service}} has not produced any logs in the last 10 minutes. Check if the service is running.

alerts.name: ftgo-unhandled-exception-alert
alerts.description: Triggers when unhandled exceptions (stack traces) are detected in any service

notify.message: Unhandled exceptions detected in service {{context.service}}: {{context.value}} exceptions with stack traces in the last 5 minutes
